||The original incarnation of miryam is a "pre-holocene" woman, the high priestess of Lemuria when it still remained above the waves. Lemuria is a more ancient civilization in this setting than Mesopotamia, for example. In this era, she also met Deborah, who was her assistant priestess and encouraged her to stay on her chosen path.

When Lemuria was destroyed, Miryam was killed along with the rest of the inhabitants. 

After thousands of years (but an unknown exact amount of time), Miryam was incarnated again as an awaker. After breaking free of her control, she returned to pursuing her objectives of resurrecting Lemuria. She also met a woman who was named and appeared identically to Deborah, who is most probably the reincarnation of the Lemurian Deborah.

Miryam fell in love with Deborah during this incarnation as well (this is confirmed in one of her dialogues). After Miryam had determined that the "Holy Child" plan was her most likely way to resurrect Lemuria, she "knew" that the parasite would have to be implanted on a sacred infant that was born at the time of correct celestial alignment.

She attempted to implant a fertilized embryo within herself multiple times (probably low-tech IVF), but it failed. Deborah took a husband during this time and became pregnant. She wanted this child of hers to become the "Holy Child". However, the time of celestial alignment came and Deborah wasn't ready to give birth, so Miryam cut her open and removed the child, who survived, but Deborah did not.

That child is Murphy, who is Deborah's biological daughter, but Miryam (at least sometimes) raised her. Other than hating Miryam for the most part due to her actions, Murphy probably doesn't know about what happened to her birth mother.||
